readme:
The <Inbox /> infrastructure for modern products

The notification platform that turns complex multi-channel delivery into a single component. Built for developers, designed for growth, powered by open source.


Learn More »

Report a bug · Docs · Website · Join our Discord · Changelog · Roadmap · X · Contact us . Recent.dev

## ⭐️ Why Novu? Novu provides a unified API that makes it simple to send notifications through multiple channels, including Inbox/In-App, Push, Email, SMS, and Chat. With Novu, you can create custom workflows and define conditions for each channel, ensuring that your notifications are delivered in the most effective way possible. ## ✨ Features - Embeddable Inbox component with real-time support - Single API for all messaging providers (Inbox/In-App, Email, SMS, Push, Chat) - Digest Engine to combine multiple notification in to a single E-mail - No-Code Block Editor for Email - Notification Workflow Engine - Embeddable user preferences component gives your subscribers control over their notifications - Community-driven ## 🚀 Getting Started [Create a free account](https://go.novu.co/dashboard?utm_source=github&utm_medium=readme&utm_campaign=create-free-account-link) and follow the instructions on the dashboard. ## 📚 Table of contents - [Getting Started](https://github.com/novuhq/novu#-getting-started) - [Embeddable Inbox and Preferences](https://github.com/novuhq/novu#embeddable-notification-center) - [Providers](https://github.com/novuhq/novu#providers) - [Email](https://github.com/novuhq/novu#-email) - [SMS](https://github.com/novuhq/novu#-sms) - [Push](https://github.com/novuhq/novu#-push) - [Chat](https://github.com/novuhq/novu#-chat) - [In-App](https://github.com/novuhq/novu#-in-app) - [Others](https://github.com/novuhq/novu#other-coming-soon) - [Need Help?](https://github.com/novuhq/novu#-need-help) - [Links](https://github.com/novuhq/novu#-links) - [License](https://github.com/novuhq/novu#%EF%B8%8F-license) ## Embeddable Inbox component Using the Novu API and admin panel, you can easily add a real-time notification center to your web app without building it yourself. You can use our [React](https://docs.novu.co/inbox/react/get-started?utm_source=github&utm_medium=readme&utm_campaign=react-starter-link), or build your own via our API and SDK. React native, Vue, and Angular are coming soon.
Read more about how to add a [notification center Inbox](https://docs.novu.co/inbox/react/get-started?utm_source=github&utm_medium=readme&utm_campaign=read-more-react-link) to your app.
## Providers Novu provides a single API to manage providers across multiple channels with a simple-to-use API and UI interface. #### 💌 Email - [x] [Sendgrid](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/email/sendgrid) - [x] [Netcore](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/email/netcore) - [x] [Mailgun](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/email/mailgun) - [x] [SES](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/email/ses) - [x] [Postmark](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/email/postmark) - [x] [Custom SMTP](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/email/nodemailer) - [x] [Mailjet](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/email/mailjet) - [x] [Mandrill](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/email/mandrill) - [x] [Brevo (formerly SendinBlue)](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/email/brevo) - [x] [MailerSend](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/email/mailersend) - [x] [Infobip](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/email/infobip) - [x] [Resend](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/email/resend) - [x] [SparkPost](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/email/sparkpost) - [x] [Outlook 365](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/email/outlook365) #### 📞 SMS - [x] [Twilio](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/sms/twilio) - [x] [Plivo](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/sms/plivo) - [x] [SNS](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/sms/sns) - [x] [Nexmo - Vonage](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/sms/nexmo) - [x] [Sms77](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/sms/sms77) - [x] [Telnyx](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/sms/telnyx) - [x] [Termii](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/sms/termii) - [x] [Gupshup](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/sms/gupshup) - [x] [SMS Central](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/sms/sms-central) - [x] [Maqsam](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/sms/maqsam) - [x] [46elks](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/sms/forty-six-elks) - [x] [Clickatell](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/sms/clickatell) - [x] [Burst SMS](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/sms/burst-sms) - [x] [Firetext](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/sms/firetext) - [x] [Infobip](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/sms/infobip) - [ ] Bandwidth - [ ] RingCentral #### 📱 Push - [x] [FCM](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/push/fcm) - [x] [Expo](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/push/expo) - [x] [APNS](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/push/apns) - [x] [OneSignal](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/push/one-signal) - [x] [Pushpad](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/push/pushpad) - [ ] Pushwoosh #### 👇 Chat - [x] [Slack](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/chat/slack) - [x] [Discord](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/chat/discord) - [x] [MS Teams](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/chat/msTeams) - [x] [Mattermost](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/tree/next/packages/providers/src/lib/chat/mattermost) #### 📱 In-App - [x] [Novu](https://docs.novu.co/inbox/react/get-started?utm_source=github&utm_medium=repository&utm_campaign=inbox-channel-link) ## 📋 Read Our Code Of Conduct Before you begin coding and collaborating, please read our [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) thoroughly to understand the standards (that you are required to adhere to) for community engagement. As part of our open-source community, we hold ourselves and other contributors to a high standard of communication. As a participant and contributor to this project, you agree to abide by our [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md). ## 💻 Need Help? We are more than happy to help you. If you are getting any errors or facing problems while working on this project, join our [Discord server](https://discord.novu.co) and ask for help. We are open to discussing anything related to the project. ## 🔗 Links - [Home page](https://novu.co?utm_source=github&utm_medium=readme&utm_campaign=main-link) - [Contribution guidelines](https://github.com/novuhq/novu/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) - [Run Novu locally](https://docs.novu.co/community/run-in-local-machine?utm_source=github&utm_medium=readme&utm_campaign=novu-locally-link) ## 🛡️ License Novu is a commercial open source company, which means some parts of this open source repository require a commercial license. The concept is called "Open Core," where the core technology is fully open source, licensed under MIT license, and the enterprise code is covered under a commercial license ("/enterprise" Enterprise Edition). Enterprise features are built by the core engineering team of Novu which is hired in full-time. The following modules and folders are licensed under the enterprise license: - `enterprise` folder at the root of the project and all of their subfolders and modules - `apps/web/src/ee` folder and all of their subfolders and modules - `apps/dashboard/src/ee` folder and all of their subfolders and modules ## 💪 Thanks to all of our contributors Thanks a lot for spending your time helping Novu grow. Keep rocking 🥂
commits:
feat(api-service,dashboard): tool progress plan cards and activity timeline fixes NV-7741 (#11213)


---COMMIT-DELIM---

prs.body:
## Summary

- **API**: Add `HandleToolProgress` usecase that manages plan card lifecycle (create → edit → finalize) via tool-use signal activities persisted in `ConversationActivity`
- **API**: Wire `onToolUseStart`, `onToolUseDone`, `onToolUseResult`, `onFinish`, `onError` webhook callbacks in `ManagedAgentService` with proper error handling (log + swallow for non-critical tool progress, log + re-throw for critical `onFinish`)
- **API**: Add `findToolActivitiesByRunId` query to `ConversationActivityRepository`; filter all SIGNAL activities from LLM conversation history
- **Dashboard**: Group tool-use signal activities by `runId` into a collapsible `ToolProgressCard` in the conversation timeline, showing tool names, statuses, and input details
- **Infra**: Bump `@novu/thalamus` to `0.1.0-alpha.8` (step-start/done events, structured `ToolResultContent`); remove local link override

## Test plan

- [ ] Send a message to a managed agent that triggers tool use (e.g. web search, bash) and verify the plan card appears in Slack with pulsing "Thinking…" animation
- [ ] Verify plan card updates as each tool completes (checkmark status)
- [ ] Verify plan card shows "Finished thinking" with all tools marked complete after agent replies
- [ ] Open conversation timeline in dashboard and verify tool-use signals are grouped into a single collapsible card per run
- [ ] Expand the card and verify tool names and input details are shown
- [ ] Verify non-tool-use signals (trigger, resolve) still render as inline log rows
- [ ] Verify no `console.log` debug output in API logs
- [ ] Verify errors from webhook handlers appear in API logs via PinoLogger


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## What changed

This PR implements end-to-end tool progress tracking for managed agents. It adds a HandleToolProgress usecase to manage plan cards through the tool lifecycle (create, update, finalize) driven by tool-use signal activities persisted during agent runs. The dashboard groups signals by runId into collapsible ToolProgressCards showing tool names and statuses. Webhook callbacks in ManagedAgentService wire tool lifecycle events to the progress handler with proper error handling.

## Affected areas

- **api**: Added HandleToolProgress usecase and refactored webhook handling in ManagedAgentService to register callbacks for tool lifecycle events (onToolUseStart, onToolUseDone, onToolUseResult, onFinish, onError); updated ChatSdkService to support plan object posting/editing and inbound callback registration; revised AgentInboundHandler to implement OnModuleInit and wire chat callbacks; simplified AgentsWebhookController to delegate to ManagedAgentService.handleWebhook.
- **dashboard**: Introduced ToolProgressCard component and updated ConversationTimeline to group tool-use signal activities by runId, rendering them as collapsible cards with tool names, statuses, and input details.
- **dal**: Extended ConversationActivityRepository with findToolActivitiesByRunId query to retrieve tool-use signals and updated createSignalActivity to capture platformMessageId.
- **workers** (enterprise): Updated thalamus-observer package.json dependency.

## Key technical decisions

- Tool progress lifecycle is driven by tool-use signal activities persisted in ConversationActivity rather than maintaining separate state.
- Plan cards are created/edited via ChatSdkService's postPlanObject/editPlanObject methods when posting/editing platform messages.
- SIGNAL-type activities are filtered from LLM conversation history to prevent tool signals from being fed back as agent context.
- Webhook handler errors are logged and swallowed for non-critical tool progress operations; onFinish errors are logged and re-thrown.
- `@novu/thalamus` updated to 0.1.0-alpha.8 (adds step-start/step-done events and structured ToolResultContent).

## Testing

The PR includes a detailed manual test plan covering tool-use triggering, plan card appearance and updates in Slack, dashboard timeline grouping behavior, and API logging validation. No new unit or e2e tests were added; verification relies on manual testing via managed agent flows in the dashboard and messaging integrations.

<!-- review_stack_entry_start -->

[![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/novuhq/novu/pull/11213?utm_source=github_walkthrough&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=change_stack)

<!-- review_stack_entry_end -->

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
prs.title: feat(api-service,dashboard): tool progress plan cards and activity timeline fixes NV-7741

prs.body: ### What changed? Why was the change needed? Agents - fixed the issue with incorrect setup step numbers. ## What changed Agent setup step numbers are now computed dynamically instead of being hardcoded. The system calculates total steps based on context (onboarding flow vs. agent details page) and agent runtime type (Claude managed vs. custom). This fixes display of incorrect step counts in the agent setup UI, ensuring users see accurate progress indicators like "1/6 SETUP AGENT BRAIN" for Claude and "1/9" for custom scaffolds. ## Affected areas **dashboard**: Agent setup components now calculate step counts dynamically. `ConnectAgentForm` computes total onboarding steps based on whether Claude is selected (6 steps) versus other connectors (9 steps). `AgentSetupSteps` derives brain steps, provider-guide steps, and handler steps from explicit constants, then passes computed `stepOffset` values to child components. `AgentCodeSetupSection` accepts a new `totalSteps` prop to render dynamic section labels instead of fixed values. ## Key technical decisions - Step calculation considers both onboarding context (presence of `connectSummary`) and agent runtime type (managed vs. custom). - Introduced explicit constants for brain steps, provider-guide reserved steps, and handler steps to make the calculation logic transparent and maintainable. ## Testing No automated tests were added. Manual verification through the UI should confirm correct step numbering displays in both onboarding flows and agent details pages. [![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/novuhq/novu/pull/11212?utm_source=github_walkthrough&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=change_stack) ### Screenshots
prs.title: fix(dashboard): agent setup numbers fixes NV-7734

prs.body:
### What changed? Why was the change needed?

Related EE repo PR: https://github.com/novuhq/packages-enterprise/pull/454

Agents - generate agent definition from the prompt.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## What changed
Added AI-powered agent generation from free-form prompts. The API exposes a new authenticated POST /agents/generate endpoint that accepts a prompt and optional runtime (managed or self-hosted) and returns a validated agent configuration (name, identifier, system prompt, tools, MCP servers, skills). The dashboard adds a prompt-driven creation flow alongside manual and connect-existing flows so users can generate, preview, adopt, or customize agent definitions produced by an LLM.

## Affected areas
- api: New controller endpoint, request/response DTOs, a GenerateManagedAgent command and use case, and a Zod schema to validate LLM output; supports AbortController-driven cancellation, runtime-specific behavior, and EE token usage tracking.
- dashboard: New hook and UI components for generation (useGenerateManagedAgent, PromptInput, GenerationStatus, AgentSuggestionPills), updates to agent create/connect flows to support prompt/manual/existing modes, and plumbing to pass generated managedOverrides into agent creation.
- dashboard (assets): Large set of new MCP provider SVG icons and a new OrbIcon used to surface suggested MCP servers in UIs.
- enterprise: Runtime integration uses the enterprise-only package `@novu/ee-ai` (lazy-loaded); PR references enterprise logic and a corresponding EE repo change.

## Key technical decisions
- `@novu/ee-ai` is loaded dynamically and the API returns ServiceUnavailable if absent to allow non-EE deployments to run without the feature.
- LLM outputs are strictly validated with a Zod schema and conservative caps (MAX_GENERATED_MCP_SERVERS=5, MAX_GENERATED_SKILLS=4) to constrain model responses.
- AbortSignal is attached to the command after construction to avoid validation/transformer issues and enable client-driven cancellation.
- Managed-agent generation is feature-flag gated (IS_MANAGED_AGENT_RUNTIME_ENABLED) and will return Forbidden when disabled.
- Token usage is recorded via EE token-tracking utilities with user/org/env/tier metadata and client abort state.

## Testing
No new automated tests included; manual verification is expected for generation flows, cancellation behavior, and feature-flag / EE-module absence handling.

<!-- review_stack_entry_start -->

[![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/novuhq/novu/pull/11206?utm_source=github_walkthrough&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=change_stack)

Release notes

Sourced from js-cookie's releases.

v3.0.7

  • Prevent cookie attribute injection: CVE-2026-46625 (eb3c40e)
  • Add Partitioned attribute to readme (b994768)
  • Publish to npm registry via trusted publisher exclusively (4dc71be)
  • Ensure consistent behaviour for get('name') + get() (1953d30)

prs.body:
### What changed? Why was the change needed? Vertical scrolling was completely blocked in the translation editor's JSON code pane. Users could not scroll through long translation files. **Root cause**: The `Editor` primitive component had `height="auto"` hardcoded on the CodeMirror instance. When `height="auto"`, CodeMirror expands to fit all its content, which means the internal `.cm-scroller` never overflows and thus never scrolls. The `height` prop was declared in the component's type and destructured, but never actually passed through to CodeMirror. **Fix**: - Use the `height` prop in the `Editor` primitive (defaults to `"auto"` for backward compatibility with other consumers) - Pass `height="100%"` from the translation `JSONEditor` so CodeMirror respects the parent container height constraint - Add `min-h-0` to flex containers in the editor panel chain so the height constraint properly cascades through the flexbox layout ### Screenshots **Before scrolling** — editor shows translation keys starting from key_001: ![Editor before scrolling](https://cursor.com/artifacts/c/art-09bc512a-903b-4e37-b4b0-3a130dc2b2e6) **After scrolling** — editor shows later keys (key_046 through key_080), proving vertical scroll works: ![Editor after scrolling](https://cursor.com/artifacts/c/art-ba472e7b-24aa-4c53-b414-0579fd0ec0b0) **Video walkthrough** of the scrolling fix in action:
Expand for optional sections ### Special notes for your reviewer This is a minimal, targeted fix. The `Editor` primitive now respects its own `height` prop (which was already declared but ignored). The default value `"auto"` preserves existing behavior for all other usages of the `Editor` component throughout the app.
Linear Issue: [NV-7716](https://linear.app/novu/issue/NV-7716/vertical-scroll-is-blocked-in-translation-editor)
  
## What changed The translation editor's vertical scrolling is now enabled by allowing CodeMirror to respect container height constraints instead of expanding indefinitely. The `Editor` primitive component now accepts and passes through a `height` prop (defaulting to `"auto"` for backward compatibility), while the translation editor explicitly sets `height="100%"` with flexbox constraints (`min-h-0`) to enforce proper height cascading through the layout hierarchy. ## Affected areas **dashboard**: Modified the `Editor` primitive component to conditionally pass the `height` prop to CodeMirror, and updated the translation editor panel (`JSONEditor` and `EditorPanel`) to apply explicit height settings and flexbox constraints that properly constrain the editor viewport. ## Testing The fix is demonstrated with before/after screenshots showing successful vertical scrolling through a long translation JSON file. No automated tests were added, as this is a UI layout fix verified through manual inspection of the behavior change. [![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/novuhq/novu/pull/11184?utm_source=github_walkthrough&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=change_stack)
prs.title: fix(dashboard): unblock vertical scroll in translation editor fixes NV-7716

prs.body:
## Summary Adds OR (and AND-of-OR) filtering for keys inside the inbox `data` filter — the API shape now mirrors `tags`. Fixes the gap raised in NV-7666: the JS client already tolerated arrays for OR, but the API rejected them (`Arrays are not supported in data filter`) and the DAL built exact-match queries instead of `$in`. ## Accepted shapes per data key | shape | semantics | | --- | --- | | `data: { status: 'open' }` | exact equality (unchanged) | | `data: { status: ['open', 'draft'] }` | OR — match any | | `data: { status: { or: ['open', 'draft'] } }` | explicit OR | | `data: { status: { and: [{ or: ['a','b'] }, { or: ['b','c'] }] } }` | AND of OR-groups (CNF) | | `data: { project: { id: ['a','b'] } }` | 1-level nested, same rules per sub-key | Across multiple top-level keys, clauses are AND-ed together — same as today. ## Walkthrough Demoed end-to-end against a local API + dashboard stack with 6 seeded `data`-tagged messages, by temporarily wiring four data-filter `tabs` into the workflow-editor test inbox. Each tab returned the expected counts (6 / 2 / 4 / 2): | Tab | Filter | Expected | Actual | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | All | none | 6 | 6 | | High priority | `data: { priority: 'high' }` | 2 | 2 | | Active | `data: { status: ['open','draft'] }` (OR) | 4 | 4 | | Targeted | `data: { status: { and: [{or:['open','draft']},{or:['draft','closed']}] } }` (CNF) | 2 | 2 | The dashboard tabs were used only for this validation and have been reverted from the PR; the recording and screenshots below stay attached as proof. ![All 4 tabs in the test inbox dropdown with badge counts](https://cursor.com/artifacts/c/art-696e394d-a896-4fac-8517-d41de32f634c) ![High priority tab — scalar filter (priority: ](https://cursor.com/artifacts/c/art-723054e7-d980-455f-9d54-f8f30310883d) ![Active tab — OR via flat array (status in \[open, draft\])](https://cursor.com/artifacts/c/art-e389c4e5-94d7-45bc-b27c-90f44354d602) ![Targeted tab — AND-of-OR (CNF) on status](https://cursor.com/artifacts/c/art-ccaf3758-7b15-4694-90dc-eee2f2c298f1) ## What changed - **`packages/shared/src/utils/data-filter.ts` (new)** — paralleling `tags-filter.ts`: - `normalizeDataFilter` — validation + flattening to CNF per dotted path - `buildDataFilterQuery` — Mongo fragment (`$and` of `$in`/equality clauses) - `checkDataFilterMatches` — in-memory match for realtime / counts - Limits mirror tags (`MAX_DATA_VALUES_PER_OR_GROUP = 100`, `MAX_DATA_AND_GROUPS = 30`, `MAX_DATA_TOTAL_VALUES = 200`), plus the existing 256-char string limit and 2-level nesting cap. - **`apps/api/src/app/inbox/utils/validate-data.ts`** — delegates to the shared helper and preserves the `BadRequestException` contract for all 4 inbox use-cases (`get` / `update-all` / `delete-all` / `mark-as-seen`). - **`libs/dal/src/repositories/message/message.repository.ts`** — `paginate`, `getFilterQueryForMessage`, `updateMessagesFromToStatus` and `deleteMessagesWithFilters` now build the data fragment with `buildDataFilterQuery` and merge it into the request `$and` via a new `mergeDataMongoFragment` helper (mirror of `mergeTagsMongoFragment`). `payload` keeps the existing flat-dot logic. - **`packages/js/src/utils/notification-utils.ts`** — `checkNotificationDataFilter` rewritten to support the same shapes. Kept inline rather than importing `@novu/shared` since `packages/js` intentionally doesn't depend on it (bundle footprint). Dangerous-key rejection (`__proto__`, `constructor`, `prototype`) kept in sync with the shared util. - **`packages/js/src/types.ts`** — docstring on `NotificationFilter.data` covering the new shapes. - **Tests** - `packages/shared/src/utils/data-filter.spec.ts` — 31 cases for `normalizeDataFilter`, `buildDataFilterQuery`, `checkDataFilterMatches`. - `packages/js/src/utils/notification-utils.test.ts` — 7 new cases for the rewritten client check. - `apps/api/src/app/inbox/e2e/get-notifications.e2e.ts` — new `data filter` block covering scalar, OR, `{or}`, `{and}` and validation errors. - `apps/api/src/app/inbox/e2e/delete-notifications.e2e.ts` — OR case for the bulk-delete path. ## Out of scope - Over-the-wire JSON shape is unchanged — clients still send a JSON-encoded `data` query param. - No SDK regeneration needed. - `GetNotificationsCountRequestDto.NotificationsFilter` still doesn't declare a `data` field even though the JS SDK sends one inside `filters`; left for a follow-up since it predates this change. - The temporary workflow-editor test-inbox tabs added in `apps/dashboard/src/components/inbox-button.tsx` for the walkthrough have been reverted — production dashboard behaviour is unchanged. ## Validation - `pnpm --filter @novu/shared` unit tests: 31/31 pass for `data-filter.spec.ts`. - `pnpm --filter @novu/js test`: 18/18 pass for `notification-utils.test.ts` (existing tags semantics + new data semantics). - `pnpm --filter @novu/shared build` and `pnpm --filter @novu/dal build` succeed. - E2E demo recorded against a local API+dashboard stack with 6 seeded `data`-tagged messages; all 4 tabs showed the expected counts (6/2/4/2). New API e2e cases were added but rely on CI to run the `#novu-v2` suite. Linear Issue: [NV-7666](https://linear.app/novu/issue/NV-7666/feature-feature-or-filtering-capability-for-data-field-keys-in)
  
## What changed Added OR and AND-of-OR (CNF) semantics for inbox `data` filters so clients can use arrays, explicit `{ or: [...] }`, and `{ and: [{ or: [...] }, ...] }` (with per-key nested objects up to one level) — matching `tags` behavior and fixing NV-7666 where array `data` filters were rejected. The change centralizes validation, query generation, and runtime matching to prevent duplication and prototype-pollution risks. ## Affected areas **shared**: New data-filter utility (normalizeDataFilter, buildDataFilterQuery, checkDataFilterMatches), types, error class, and strict validation/enforcement (limits: 100 values per OR group, 30 AND groups, 200 total values, 256-char strings, max 2 nesting levels). **api**: Inbox validation now delegates to shared normalization and translates validation failures into BadRequestException; added buildInboxDataFilterQuery wrapper for repository usage; tests updated to exercise data-filtered get/delete endpoints. **dal**: Message repository merges the shared-built data filter Mongo fragments (CNF/$and-shaped) into queries used by paginate, getFilterQueryForMessage, updateMessagesFromToStatus, and deleteMessagesWithFilters. **js**: Rewrote local checkNotificationDataFilter to the same CNF semantics (kept local to avoid adding the shared dependency); added JSDoc to NotificationFilter.data and aligned client-side rejection of dangerous keys with server. **dashboard**: Added workflow-editor test-inbox tabs demonstrating the new data-filter shapes (All, High priority, Active, Targeted). ## Key technical decisions - Centralize validation and Mongo query generation in `shared` to avoid duplication and ensure consistent security rules (prototype/dot/dollar key rejection). - Emit CNF-style Mongo fragments (top-level `$and`) so fragments can be merged into existing query builders without changing API payloads. - Keep client JS matcher local to avoid dependency churn while aligning security checks with shared. ## Testing Added shared Vitest suite (31 cases) for normalization, query building, and matching; extended JS unit tests (~18 cases) and added inbox e2e tests for get/delete data filtering. Builds for shared and dal pass; e2e runs noted but some were not executed locally due to unrelated Mongo issues. [![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/novuhq/novu/pull/11135?utm_source=github_walkthrough&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=change_stack)
prs.title: feat(shared): OR/AND filtering for `data` in inbox useNotifications fixes NV-7666

prs.body:
## Summary

- Upload inbound email attachments to S3 at SMTP ingest time so the BullMQ queue payload carries only slim metadata (`filename`, `contentType`, `size`, `url`, `storagePath`) instead of raw binary blobs — significantly reducing Redis memory consumption.
- Add `AttachmentRehydrator` service that re-downloads attachments from S3 for legacy webhook consumers (domain-route + reply-to), embedding deprecated `content`/`contentBytes` fields for one transition release.
- Agent webhooks (already URL-based) receive the new `size` field additively — no rehydration needed.

## Data flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant SMTP as SMTP Server
    participant S3
    participant Queue as BullMQ (Redis)
    participant Worker
    participant S3b as S3 (rehydrate)
    participant WH as Legacy Webhook
    participant Agent as Agent Webhook

    SMTP->>S3: PUT attachment binary
    S3-->>SMTP: presigned GET URL
    SMTP->>Queue: slim metadata {filename, contentType, size, url, storagePath}
    Queue->>Worker: dequeue job
    Worker->>S3b: GET binary (legacy paths only)
    S3b-->>Worker: attachment bytes
    Worker->>WH: payload with url + size + deprecated content
    Worker->>Agent: payload with url + size (no content)
```

## Breaking changes

- `InboundEmailAttachment.content` and `.contentBytes` are marked `@deprecated` — they will be removed in the next major version. Consumers should migrate to downloading via the `url` field.
- The queue payload shape changed from raw `mailparser` attachment objects to `IInboundParseAttachment`. This is an internal interface, not user-facing.

## New environment variables

| Variable | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `S3_REGION` | Yes | `''` | AWS region for the S3 bucket |
| `S3_BUCKET_NAME` | Yes | `''` | S3 bucket name (leave empty to disable — attachments will be dropped) |
| `S3_LOCAL_STACK` | No | `''` | Override endpoint for LocalStack/MinIO |
| `CDN_URL` | No | `''` | CDN prefix for presigned URLs |
| `INBOUND_ATTACHMENT_URL_TTL_SECONDS` | No | `604800` | Presigned URL TTL (max 7 days) |
| `INBOUND_FAIL_ON_ATTACHMENT_UPLOAD_ERROR` | No | `false` | SMTP-reject on upload failure vs. drop and continue |

**Ops note:** An S3 bucket lifecycle policy (expiration >= TTL) is required for the `inbound-mail/*` prefix.

## Test plan

- [ ] `attachment-uploader.spec.ts` — 8 unit tests: Buffer upload, JSON-Buffer format, S3 failures, missing bucket, TTL capping
- [ ] `attachment-rehydrator.spec.ts` — 7 unit tests: rehydration, NonExistingFileError, parallel processing, mixed failures  
- [ ] `inbound-email-parse.spec.ts` — reply-to webhook carries both new `url`/`size` and legacy `content`
- [ ] `domain-route.strategy.spec.ts` — slim metadata (no binary) passes through to delivery layer
- [ ] TypeScript compiles cleanly across `apps/inbound-mail`, `apps/worker`, `libs/application-generic`

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## What changed

Inbound email attachments are offloaded to S3 at SMTP ingest so queued jobs carry slim metadata (filename, contentType, size, url, storagePath) instead of raw binary blobs, reducing Redis/BullMQ memory usage. An AttachmentRehydrator service downloads binaries from storage for legacy webhook consumers (domain-route and reply-to) so existing integrations continue to work; agent webhooks remain URL-based and also receive a size field. Upload failures can optionally surface as transient SMTP errors (451) to trigger sender retries.

## Affected areas

**inbound-mail**: Adds deterministic S3 uploader producing presigned GET URLs, replaces raw attachments with slim metadata in queued messages, validates S3-related env vars, and can fail SMTP delivery on partial upload failures when enabled.  
**worker**: Queue payloads now use IInboundParseAttachment[]; InboundEmailParse command validates nested attachment DTOs; reply-to and domain-route strategies use AttachmentRehydrator to rehydrate legacy content while agent webhooks use URL+size.  
**application-generic**: Adds AttachmentRehydrator that fetches bytes via StorageService.getFile and returns InboundEmailAttachment objects (including deprecated content/contentBytes when available); inbound-domain-route delivery updated to accept rehydrated attachments.  
**api**: Registers AttachmentRehydrator in the domains/usecases provider array to fix DI resolution for inbound-domain-route delivery.  
**shared**: Adds InboundEmailAttachment type (filename, contentType, size, url, storagePath) and documents deprecated optional content/contentBytes; NovuEmailAttachment keeps url for agent compatibility.

## Key technical decisions

- New dependency: @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner added (with existing AWS SDK clients) to create presigned GET URLs.  
- Breaking/upgrade notes: InboundEmailAttachment.content and .contentBytes marked @deprecated (to be removed next major); internal queue payload shape changed to IInboundParseAttachment.  
- Config: new envs S3_REGION, S3_BUCKET_NAME (empty disables uploads), S3_LOCAL_STACK, CDN_URL, INBOUND_ATTACHMENT_URL_TTL_SECONDS (default 604800, max 7 days), INBOUND_FAIL_ON_ATTACHMENT_UPLOAD_ERROR (default false).  
- Ops: require S3 lifecycle policy for inbound-mail/* with expiration ≥ TTL to avoid unbounded storage growth.

## Testing

Adds unit tests for the uploader (success, failures, TTL cap, deterministic keys, input-shape guards) and for AttachmentRehydrator (concurrency and per-item error handling), updates worker specs to assert rehydration behavior, and relies on TypeScript compilation checks across affected apps/libs.

<!-- review_stack_entry_start -->

[![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/novuhq/novu/pull/11053)

<!-- review_stack_entry_end -->
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
prs.title: feat(inbound-mail): offload email attachments to S3 to reduce Redis memory fixes NV-7616

prs.body:
- Added `WorkflowRunCountRepository` to fetch total workflow runs count from the database. - Enhanced `BuildWorkflowRunsCountChart` use case to conditionally build counts based on feature flag. - Updated `GetCharts` use case to include workflow runs count in chart data. - Modified analytics page to calculate and display workflow runs count. - Removed mock data for workflow runs count as it is now fetched from the repository.### What changed? Why was the change needed? ## What changed This PR simplifies analytics by removing feature-flag-driven branching across multiple use cases and consolidating data retrieval to rollup queries, deletes several legacy ClickHouse query methods, and removes ANALYTIC_V2 feature flags. It also adds a WorkflowRunCountRepository and updates analytics endpoints and the analytics page to fetch and display real workflow runs counts instead of using mock data. ## Affected areas - **api**: Analytics use cases were simplified to use rollup-based repositories; a new WorkflowRunCountRepository was added and GetCharts now includes workflow runs count returned to callers. - **dashboard**: Analytics page updated to calculate and display actual workflow runs count (mock data removed). - **shared**: Nine ANALYTIC_V2 feature flag keys were removed from FeatureFlagsKeysEnum. - **application-generic**: Deprecated analytics query methods removed from StepRunRepository, TraceLogRepository, and WorkflowRunRepository. - **enterprise (ee)**: Minor import/format changes in an enterprise e2e test file were made (no logic changes). - **agents/domains/providers**: Small import reformatting and non-functional adjustments. ## Key technical decisions - Standardize analytics on rollup repositories (TraceRollup and related rollup counts) and remove fallback paths to older per-event repositories to simplify logic and maintenance. - Remove ANALYTIC_V2 feature flags and the conditional feature-flag branching that selected between old/new query implementations. ## Testing No new tests were added; some spec files only had formatting changes. Recommend validating analytics behavior via existing integration/e2e tests and manual verification of the dashboard analytics page to ensure workflow runs count displays correctly. [![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/novuhq/novu/pull/10595?utm_source=github_walkthrough&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=change_stack) ### Screenshots
Expand for optional sections ### Related enterprise PR ### Special notes for your reviewer
